steps.title: Spot the paradox at the core
steps.content: The passage describes a paradox: new, more precise coordinates were calculated over decades, yet the prime meridian (defined by older data) wasn't actually changed. Better information didn't lead to the expected action. The transition must acknowledge this contradiction rather than simply narrate the events.

steps.title: Choose the word that frames the paradox
steps.content: Granted is a concession word that sets up a contrast structure: “I acknowledge X, yet Y [surprisingly] happened instead.” It directly frames the paradox—“Granted that new precise coordinates emerged, yet the meridian stayed put”—showing why this situation is noteworthy and leading naturally to the eventual 1980s resolution. The others don't capture this structure: Specifically merely narrates detail, Again and again stresses repetition without addressing the paradox, and To that end wrongly implies purposeful causality instead of inaction. That matches choice C — C is correct.
